description: Lists properties and responses of geogrid elements in the range. If no keyword is given, the default output of the command is that associated with the information keyword.

6.0.description: List properties and response of each geogrid element in the range.

keywords.name: apply
keywords.description: uniform applied pressure acting on the element

keywords.name: coupling
keywords.description: Information about the shear coupling springs connecting the liner to the zones. The confining stress, displacement, yield state, and direction is given.

keywords.name: depth-factor
keywords.description: depth factor used during stress recovery

keywords.name: element-type
keywords.description: finite element type used

keywords.name: group
keywords.description: list the group assignment(s) of this element. If slot is supplied, list the group assignment for this element, if any, in slot s.

keywords.name: information
keywords.description: Lists general element information, including id and component-id numbers, connected nodes, centroids, surface area, volume, and the hide and select status.

keywords.name: force-node
keywords.description: List nodal forces. By default, components are displayed in terms of the element local coordinate systems. These are the forces exerted by the nodes on the element.

keywords.name: force-node-local
keywords.description: List forces in terms of the element local coordinate system (the default).

keywords.name: force-node-global
keywords.description: List forces in terms of the global coordinate system.

keywords.name: property-density
keywords.description: density, ρ

keywords.name: property-thickness
keywords.description: geogrid thickness

keywords.name: property-thermal-expansion
keywords.description: thermal expansion coefficient

keywords.name: property-anisotropic-membrane
keywords.description: anisotropic membrane material-stiffness coefficients { cʹ11, cʹ12, cʹ13, cʹ22, cʹ23, cʹ33 }

keywords.name: property-anisotropic-bending
keywords.description: anisotropic bending material-stiffness coefficients { cʹ11, cʹ12, cʹ13, cʹ22, cʹ23, cʹ33 }

keywords.name: property-anisotropic-both
keywords.description: anisotropic bending and membrane material-stiffness coefficients { cʹ11, cʹ12, cʹ13, cʹ22, cʹ23, cʹ33 }

keywords.name: property-material-x
keywords.description: Vector used to determine the local X direction of the material coordinate system.

keywords.name: property-isotropic
keywords.description: isotropic material properties: E and v, where E is Young’s modulus [F/L2] and v is Poisson’s ratio

keywords.name: property-orthotropic-membrane
keywords.description: orthotropic membrane material-stiffness coefficients { cʹ11, cʹ12, cʹ22, cʹ33 }

keywords.name: property-orthotropic-bending
keywords.description: orthotropic membrane material-stiffness coefficients { cʹ11, cʹ12, cʹ22, cʹ33 }

keywords.name: property-orthotropic-both
keywords.description: orthotropic membrane material-stiffness coefficients { cʹ11, cʹ12, cʹ22, cʹ33 }

keywords.name: property-coupling-cohesion
keywords.description: coupling spring cohesion (stress units)

keywords.name: property-coupling-friction
keywords.description: coupling spring friction angle (degrees)

keywords.name: property-coupling-stiffness
keywords.description: coupling spring stiffness per unit area

keywords.name: property-slide
keywords.description: large-strain sliding flag

keywords.name: property-slide-tolerance
keywords.description: large-strain sliding tolerance

keywords.name: system-local
keywords.description: element local coordinate system

keywords.description: List nodal forces. By default, components are displayed in terms of the element local coordinate systems. These are the forces exerted by the nodes on the element.

keywords.name: property-slide
keywords.description: large-strain sliding flag

keywords.name: property-slide-tolerance
keywords.description: large-strain sliding tolerance

keywords.name: system-local
keywords.description: element local coordinate system

keywords.name: apply
keywords.description: uniform applied pressure acting on the element

keywords.name: coupling
keywords.description: information about the shear coupling springs connecting the geogrid to the target object (zones in FLAC3D and 3DEC, and particles in PFC3D): confinement, displacement, stress, yield state and shear direction. Displacement and stress are always positive and act in the geogrid tangent plane; and shear direction is given as a unit vector in the global system.

keywords.name: depth-factor
keywords.description: depth factor used during stress recovery for elements with an elastic material model

keywords.name: element-type
keywords.description: finite element type

keywords.name: force-node-global
keywords.description: generalized nodal forces (force and moment) acting on the element. These are the generalized forces exerted by the nodes on the element. express the values in the element coordinate system (the default) express the values in the global coordinate system

keywords.name: group
keywords.description: group assignment(s). If slot is specified, then list the group assignment, if any, in slot s .

keywords.name: information
keywords.description: general element information: nodal connectivity, centroid, surface area, volume, and hide/select status

keywords.name: model
keywords.description: constitutive model

keywords.name: plastic-state
keywords.description: Plasticity state indicators. Each shell element with a plastic material model has a number of integration points distributed throughout its volume, and each of them tracks its plasticity state. The plasticity states at all integration points are listed, unless integration-point is specified. The integration point locations are shown here . The plasticity states of the plastic constitutive models can be listed by the structure geogrid cmodel list states command.

keywords.name: plastic-stress
keywords.description: Stress for elements with a plastic material model. Each shell element with a plastic material model has a number of integration points distributed throughout its volume, and each of them tracks its stress. The stress state satisfies the plane-stress assumption such that the only non-zero components are \(\sigma_{xx}\) , \(\sigma_{yy}\) and \(\sigma_{xy}\) . The stress is expressed in the element coordinate system, unless surface is specified, in which case the stress is expressed in the surface coordinate system (which must already be established for the shell element — see the structure geogrid recover surface command). The stresses at all integration points are listed, unless integration-point is specified. The integration point locations are shown here .

keywords.name: property-slide-tolerance
keywords.description: The properties consist of constitutive model properties, shell-type properties, and coupling-spring properties. The constitutive model properties are listed by specifying the constitutive property name; these names are not listed here. Elastic-plastic constitutive model properties are stored at each integration point. Such properties are the same at all integration points, except for the softening properties of the strain-softening/hardening Mohr Coulomb constitutive model. The value at a particular integration point is listed by specifying the integration-point keyword — the integration point layout is shown here . Shell-Type Properties mass density thermal-expansion coefficient shell thickness Coupling-Spring Properties coupling spring cohesion (stress units) coupling spring friction angle (degrees) coupling spring stiffness per unit area large-strain sliding flag large-strain sliding tolerance

keywords.name: resultants
keywords.description: stress resultants expressed in the surface coordinate system. By default, the values are given at the element centroid; however, if nodes is specified, then the values are given at the element nodes. The stress resultants are recovered by the structure geogrid recover resultants command.

keywords.name: stress
keywords.description: stresses at the indicated depth expressed in the global coordinate system for elements with an elastic material model. By default, the values are given at the element centroid; however, if nodes is specified, then the values are given at the element nodes. The stresses are recovered by the structure geogrid recover stress command.

keywords.name: stress-bounds
keywords.description: minimum and maximum stress within the element. If the element has an elastic material model, then uses the 2D principal stresses at the element centroid at the shell surfaces (obtained from the nodal forces acting on the element, and with no nodal smoothing). If the element has a plastic material model, then the 2D principal stresses are found at the element centroid of each integration-point layer, and the minimum and maximum values over all layers are listed.

keywords.name: stress-principal
keywords.description: principal stresses at the indicated depth expressed in the global coordinate system for elements with an elastic material model. By default, the values are given at the element centroid; however, if nodes is specified, then the values are given at the element nodes. The stresses are recovered by the structure geogrid recover stress command.

keywords.name: system-local
keywords.description: element local coordinate system

keywords.name: system-material
keywords.description: element material coordinate system (used if the element has orthotropic or anisotropic elastic material properties)
